misconceptionSummary: Students can continue a geometric sequence but do not understand what makes it different from a linear one.

visuals.ruleText: A geometric sequence has a constant multiplier
visuals.subText: Each term is found by multiplying the previous term

steps.narration: That is the defining idea of a geometric sequence.

lines.text: Geometric: multiply by the same number

steps.narration: This is the key contrast with linear sequences.

lines.text: 2, 4, 8, 16 grows faster because the multiplier acts on a larger term each time

steps.narration: Repeated multiplication causes the size of the jumps to change.

lines.text: Same multiplier = same ratio between consecutive terms

steps.narration: Another way to describe a geometric sequence is by its constant ratio.

visuals.expression: Geometric sequences are built by repeated multiplication
visuals.label: Constant ratio, not constant difference

steps.narration: The conceptual key is multiplicative growth.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: thinking every evenly increasing-looking sequence is linear. Geometric growth is driven by ratio, not difference.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: In a geometric sequence the jump size changes, but the multiplier stays the same.
